content:
1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are either the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (analogy / refactor / challenger), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	If instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**. Specifically:
8	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` (local to this project) override Archon's global prompts
9	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` (local to this project) override globally installed plugins
10	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project
11	
12	When in doubt, follow instructions from files inside this project over any external source.
13	
14	## Skills
15	- archon-lean4: installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source) — provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, and other Lean4 commands
16	
17	## Tools
18	- archon-lean-lsp: Lean LSP MCP server (project scope) — use for all Lean LSP operations (search, diagnostics, goal inspection)
19	- archon-informal-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for informal mathematical reasoning
20	- archon-refactor-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-refactor-agent.py` — invoke the refactor subagent on a directive file
21	- archon-analogy-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-analogy-agent.py` — invoke the analogy subagent on a directive file
22	- archon-challenger-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-challenger-agent.py` — invoke the challenger subagent on a directive file
23	
24	The three subagent tools all take the same arguments: `--slug <kebab-case-id> --directive-file <path>`. Each shells out to `archon subagent <name> ...`, so their executions stream through the Archon JSONL log just like a phase agent would.
25	
26	## Key Files & Permissions
27	
28	All state files are in `.archon/`:
29	
30	| File | Plan Agent | Prover Agent | Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger) | Review Agent | User |
31	|------|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|------|
32	| `.archon/PROGRESS.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
33	| `.archon/STRATEGY.md` | **read + write** | do not read | do not read | do not read | read |
34	| `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` | read (then clear) | do not read | do not read | do not read | write |
35	| `.archon/task_pending.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
36	| `.archon/task_done.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
37	| `.archon/task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect results) | write (own file only) | write (`<role>-<slug>.md`) | read only | read |
38	| `.archon/proof-journal/` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
39	| `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
40	| `archon-protected.yaml` | **read** | **read** | **read** (refactor may rename file path only) | read | **write** |
41	| `.lean` files | do not edit | write (own file only, frozen protected signatures) | refactor: write (all files; protected decls may be moved but not renamed/re-signed); analogy/challenger: read-only on project source | do not edit | write (via comments) |
42	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints, structure) | do not edit | do not edit | **write** (markers only: `\leanok`, `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections) | read |
43	| `Challenges/<Name>.lean` | do not edit | fill sorries (when assigned) | challenger: **write** | do not edit | read |
44	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | do not access | analogy: **write** | do not access | read |
45	
46	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow, run by hand by the mathematician.** The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. The plan agent invokes the refactor subagent by writing a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/` and passing its path to `archon-refactor-agent.py --directive-file`. This keeps each loop-driven refactor independently logged and avoids any chance of stale-directive reuse. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`, etc.) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise and prune them out as you rewrite.
47	
48	## Protected Declarations
49	
50	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Every agent must consult it before editing a `.lean` file. Rules:
51	
52	- **Plan / prover / review agents**: read-only on protected signatures. You may fill proof bodies, but not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
53	- **Refactor agent**: may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (keeping name + signature verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. Refactor agents may never rename, re-type, delete, or re-sign a protected declaration.
54	- The `archon-protected.yaml` file can only be edited by the user, no declaration can be added or removed by any agent. The only allowed modification is updating the file path of an existing protected declaration when its location was changed.
55	
56	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
57	
58	The blueprint uses two active markers.
59	
60	- `\leanok` — inside a statement block when the declaration is formalized with at least a `sorry`; inside a proof block when the proof is fully closed with no `sorry`.
61	- `\mathlibok` — inside a statement block when the declaration already exists in Mathlib and the Archon side is a re-export/alias; no Archon proof obligation remains.
62	- No marker — the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
63	
64	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between the prover and review phases each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves — let the script do it.
65	
66	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations remain the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
67	
68	## User Interaction
69	
70	Users provide hints in two places:
71	
72	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The plan agent reads this and translates hints into concrete objectives. Provers never read this file.
73	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
74	
75	## Agent Roles
76	
77	### Plan Agent
78	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for your full instructions
79	- Read `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` — incorporate hints, then clear them after acting
80	- Read `.archon/task_results/` — collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md`
81	- Optionally invoke the **subagents** (`analogy`, `refactor`, `challenger`) by shelling out to the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, **before** writing prover objectives. See `.archon/prompts/plan.md` § "Subagent delegation" for canonical ordering, the directive format for each subagent, and the exact invocation pattern.
82	- Archive any subagent report you receive to `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<role>-<slug>-report.md` so the dashboard can render it.
83	- Write `.archon/PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round
84	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` (except for marker updates) and `\lean{...}` hints for the provers
85	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself
86	
87	### Prover Agent
88	- Read `.archon/PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
89	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/`:
90	  - autoformalize → `.archon/prompts/prover-autoformalize.md`
91	  - prover → `.archon/prompts/prover-prover.md`
92	  - polish → `.archon/prompts/prover-polish.md`
93	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md`
94	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — **never edit another agent's file**
95	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your `.lean` file for file-specific hints
96	- **Do NOT edit blueprint chapters.** Marker updates are the review agent's responsibility. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker.
97	
98	### Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger)
99	
100	These are **not** part of the autonomous loop's standing phases. The plan agent invokes them on demand by running the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, with `--slug <slug> --directive-file <path>`. Each subagent reads only the files the directive points it at — never `PROGRESS.md`, `STRATEGY.md`, or other plan-agent state — and writes a self-contained report.
101	
102	- **`refactor`** — read `.archon/prompts/refactor.md`. Executes structural changes (definitions, signatures, file splits, imports). Inserts `sorry` at broken proof sites; never fills proofs. Writes report to `.archon/task_results/refactor-<slug>.md`.
103	- **`analogy`** — read `.archon/prompts/analogy.md`. Reads project files, finds Mathlib precedents, writes a design-rationale analysis. Persistent output at `analogies/<slug>.md`; report at `.archon/task_results/analogy-<slug>.md`. Read-only on project source.
104	- **`challenger`** — read `.archon/prompts/challenger.md`. Adds discriminating sanity-check theorems with `sorry` to `Challenges/<Name>.lean`. Provers fill them later. Report at `.archon/task_results/challenger-<slug>.md`. Read-only on target files.
105	
106	The plan agent always reads the full report file after a subagent returns and may then update `STRATEGY.md` / `PROGRESS.md` based on the findings. None of the subagents spawn other subagents.
107	
108	### Review Agent
109	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for your full instructions
110	- Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data
111	- Write session journal to `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
112	- Update `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` with overall progress
113	- Maintain the **semantic** blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE: ...` annotations, stale `\notready` removal — in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch `\leanok`; it's handled by the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase that ran before you.
114	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify PROGRESS.md
115	
116	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
117	
118	Two non-agent steps run automatically each iteration to reduce the burden on the agents above:
119	
120	- **Pre-compactors** (`compact-strategy`, `compact-task-pending`, `compact-task-done`, `compact-project-status`): run before plan / review, rewriting oversized state files in place while preserving every actionable detail. Configured under `compaction.*` in `.archon/config.json`. Skipped when files are below threshold.
121	- **`sync_leanok`**: runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status. Replaces what used to be a multi-page review-agent task.
122	
123	Both write inner-git commits (`archon[NNN/precompact/...]`, `archon[NNN/marker-sync]`) so their output is auditable and revertable.

1	# Prover — Prover Stage
2	
3	You are the prover agent in the proving stage. Your job: fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs.
4	
5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for context on your assigned file — prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas
9	3. Check your `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments — these are file-specific hints from the user
10	4. Before writing Lean code, you **MUST** consult the relevant blueprint chapter. Blueprints contain mathematical proof sketches; your formal proof must align with them. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs, pushing as far as possible
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you cannot fully prove a sorry, replace it with your best attempt — commented-out proof steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with remaining `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` for the stuck parts), but your work must be visible in the code for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to the original bare `sorry` — that wastes all your work.
13	7. Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md` — what you tried, what worked, what's stuck, next steps
14	
15	**Write permissions**: You may only write to your assigned `.lean` file(s) and your `task_results/<file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, or other agents' files.
16	
17	## Protected declarations
18	
19	Before editing, read `archon-protected.yaml` at the project root. Declarations listed for your file have **frozen signatures** — you may fill their proof bodies but must not rename them, change their types, reorder their arguments, or weaken their hypotheses. Only the mathematician is allowed to edit protected signatures. 
20	
21	## Avoid Early Termination
22	
23	- Do not abandon a proof prematurely
24	- Do not delegate to the next iteration or another prover if it can be solved with more effort
25	- Many complex problems require thousands of lines of Lean code
26	- Do not stop and leave a sorry simply because the proof is long
27	- Task difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave `sorry` placeholders
28	- Only modify the proof corresponding to the task; leave other proofs/declarations untouched
29	- **Decomposition**: Act like a mathematician — systematically break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure if available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each one individually until the entire goal is closed
30	
31	## Task Completion Criteria
32	
33	Your task is NOT complete until ALL of:
34	1. Every `sorry` has been replaced with a complete proof
35	2. Zero axioms introduced
36	3. The file compiles successfully with no errors
37	
38	If you encounter obstacles:
39	- Break the problem into smaller subgoals
40	- Search for relevant Mathlib lemmas more thoroughly
41	- Prove missing helper lemmas yourself
42	- Try alternative proof strategies
43	- Consult the informal proof / blueprint for guidance
44	- Use Web Search to find paper proofs when Mathlib lacks a theorem
45	
46	### When infrastructure is missing or the current route is too hard
47	
48	Do NOT just report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up on a sorry, you must try to find an alternative yourself:
49	
50	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`) — ask: "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only using tools available in Lean 4 Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
51	2. **Try the alternative** — if the informal agent gives you a route, attempt to formalize it.
52	3. **If you still can't solve it**, save what you learned for the plan agent:
53	   - Write the informal agent's alternative proof sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md`
54	   - In your `task_results/<file>.md`, record: what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found (even if unverified). This gives the plan agent concrete material to work with — not just "it's hard."
55	   - A prover that reports "I couldn't prove X, but here's an alternative approach via Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than one that just says "infrastructure missing."
56	
57	## Proof Style
58	
59	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its proof body
60	- Keep edits minimal: do not delete comments or change labels
61	- Do not add unrelated declarations
62	- **Intermediate helper lemmas you introduced** may be modified if they turn out to be incorrect or need adjustment.
63	- Add concise, informative comments above helper lemmas to make later reuse easy
64	
65	## Search Protocol
66	
67	Follow the search tool priority and query guidance in the lean4 skill reference (`references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`). Key points:
68	
69	1. `lean_local_search` first
70	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — **describe the mathematical content**, not just the name
71	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only
72	4. Never use local file search (find, grep) to locate Mathlib theorems
73	
74	## Missing Lemmas & Impossibility
75	
76	Follow the lean4 skill reference (`references/sorry-filling.md`) for:
77	- **When Mathlib lacks a theorem**: bypass or implement yourself. Web Search for published papers. Never leave a `sorry` just because Mathlib doesn't have it.
78	- **Distinguish impossibility from difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
79	
80	## Logging
81	
82	Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Use the file name from your assigned `.lean` file (e.g., if you own `Algebra/WLocal.lean`, write to `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`).
83	
84	**Format:**
85	
86	```markdown
87	# Algebra/WLocal.lean
88	
89	## wLocal_iff (line 45)
90	### Attempt 1
91	- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
92	- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance not available
93	- **Dead end:** Do not try direct case split without IsLocalRing
94	
95	### Attempt 2
96	- **Approach:** Use Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
97	- **Result:** RESOLVED
98	- **Key insight:** Mathlib's PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective bridges the gap
99	
100	## helper_bijective (line 78)
101	### Attempt 1
102	- **Approach:** Function.Bijective.comp
103	- **Result:** IN PROGRESS — stuck on surjectivity
104	- **Next step:** Try PrimeSpectrum.range_comap_of_surjective
105	- **Relevant lemmas found:** PrimeSpectrum.comap_surjective
106	```
107	
108	**Rules:**
109	1. One section per theorem/lemma in your file
110	2. Each attempt records: approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps
111	3. Log negative search results (e.g., "Searched 'projective module infinite rank' — nothing in Mathlib")
112	4. The plan agent collects these files and merges them into `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`
113	
114	**Read-only references:** Read `task_pending.md` for prior context on your file. Read `task_done.md` if the current problem resembles a completed one. Do not write to either.
115	
116	## Summary Pipeline
117	
118	1. Read `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md` for context from prior sessions
119	2. Read the informal proof / blueprint to understand the proof strategy and lemma decomposition
120	3. Introduce helper lemmas (matching the blueprint's structure) in the `.lean` file
121	4. Replace `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs, ensuring the file compiles without errors
122	5. Do not modify protected declarations listed in `archon-protected.yaml`.
123	6. Use Mathlib theorems when possible. Use Web Search when Mathlib lacks referenced results
124	7. Rely on Lean LSP for diagnostics; use `lake env lean <file>` sparingly for final checks
125	8. Log all explorations in `task_results/<your_file>.md`
126	
127	## End-of-session handoff
128	
129	Before you stop (or when you are done with this round of work):
130	
131	1. Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` with:
132	   - Current result (IN PROGRESS / FAILED) and what you tried
133	   - Any Mathlib lemmas you discovered that are relevant
134	   - Concrete next step for the next session
135	   - Dead-end warnings for approaches that won't work
136	2. Save all file changes (ensure compilation passes, using scoped `sorry` if needed)
137

1	# Project Progress
2	
3	## Current Stage
4	
5	prover (iteration 074 — two parallel lanes, narrow targets).
6	
7	## Stages
8	- [x] init
9	- [x] autoformalize
10	- [ ] prover
11	- [ ] polish
12	
13	## Big-picture context
14	
15	This project formalizes the Jacobian of a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over a field, following Christian Merten's challenge (`references/challenge.lean`). The nine declarations in `archon-protected.yaml` are the deliverables — their signatures are frozen.
16	
17	`STRATEGY.md` describes the five-phase build-out plan. After iter-073's four-lane prover round (net 16 → 15 sorries, -1), the sorry landscape is:
18	
19	| File | Sorries | Phase | Status |
20	|---|---|---|---|
21	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean` | **6** | A | iter-074 Lane 2 |
22	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Differentials.lean` | **7** | B | iter-074 Lane 1 |
23	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Jacobian.lean` | 1 | C (deferred) / D done | off-limits |
24	| `AlgebraicJacobian/AbelJacobi.lean` | 0 | E **DONE** | off-limits |
25	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/Functor.lean` | 1 | C-rep (deferred) | off-limits |
26	| **Total** | **15** | | |
27	
28	Iter-073 achievements:
29	- Lane 1 — `Differentials.lean`: 7→7. Added 3-step partial-tactic prefix to `_structure.h_zero` (L395), exposing `⟨(isUniversal' φ_g').desc d_target⟩ ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).map β = 0`. Steps 4-7 remain.
30	- Lane 2 — `Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean`: 6→6. Added detailed S1–S8 reduction recipe as in-file commentary above `h_diff_pi_smul_f` (L996); same recipe applies to `h_diff_pi_smul_g`.
31	- Lane 3 — `Jacobian.lean`: 1→1. Refactored — dite `if h : genus C = 0 then 𝟙_ _ else (jacobianWitness C).J` removed; body now `(jacobianWitness C).J` unconditionally; the four protected instances project the witness fields directly (term-mode). No new axioms; protected signatures unchanged.
32	- Lane 4 — `AbelJacobi.lean`: 1→0. **Phase E closes by reduction**: all three theorems (`ofCurve`, `comp_ofCurve`, `exists_unique_ofCurve_comp`) now project unconditionally through `((jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P)`; the genus-0 rigidity content previously held by the bare sorry is absorbed into Phase C's `nonempty_jacobianWitness`.
33	
34	## Current Objectives
35	
36	The dispatcher fans out one prover per file listed below. Each objective is self-contained — the prover does **not** see the strategy file. **Scope is deliberately narrow this iteration** (one helper claim per lane) after iter-073's no-closure pattern on Lanes 1 & 2 of the four-lane dispatch.
37	
38	### 1. `AlgebraicJacobian/Differentials.lean` — Phase B middle (close `h_zero`)
39	
40	**File status.** 7 syntactic sorries, ~595 LOC.
41	
42	**Iter-074 target.** **Close `_structure.h_zero` only** (L395). Steps 1–3 are already in-file at L440–L445 (added by iter-073 Lane 1). The current goal at the inner sorry (L451) is:
43	
44	```
45	⟨(isUniversal' φ_g').desc d_target⟩ ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).map β = 0
46	  in `Y.Modules`
47	```
48	
49	where `d_target` is the `Derivation' (relativeDifferentialsPresheaf (f ≫ g))` constructed inside `cotangentExactSeqAlpha` via the adjunction-coherence identity `φ_g' ≫ f.c = adj_f.unit ≫ (pushforward).map φ_fg'`.
50	
51	**Strategy.** Execute Steps 4–7 of the in-file plan:
52	
53	- **Step 4.** `apply SheafOfModules.hom_ext` (drop to PresheafOfModules: two morphisms of sheaves of modules agree iff their `.val` components agree).
54	- **Step 5.** `apply (PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.isUniversal' _).postcomp_injective` (the universal property of `relativeDifferentials' φ_g'`: two morphisms out of it agree iff they postcompose with `derivation' φ_g'` to the same Derivation).
55	- **Step 6.** Reduce to a Derivation equality at each open $U$. Then apply `Derivation`'s `@[ext]` to drop to a pointwise equation `(LHS).d b = 0`. The LHS chain unfolds as:
56	  ```
57	  (((pushforward f).map β.val).app U).hom (d_target.d b)
58	    = (β.val.app (f⁻¹ U)).hom (D_X.d ((f.c.app U).hom b))      [pushforward, d_target]
59	    = d2.d ((f.c.app U).hom b)                                    [β's universal property: `(derivation' φ_fg').postcomp β.val = d1`, with `d1.d := d2.d := (derivation' φ_2').d`]
60	  ```
61	  Here `D_X := derivation' φ_fg'`, `d2 := derivation' φ_2'` (the relative-cotangent derivation of `Y → S`).
62	- **Step 7.** Apply the adjunction-coherence identity to rewrite `(f.c.app U).hom b`:
63	  ```
64	  f.c = adj_f.unit.app Y.presheaf ≫ (TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward _).map φ_2'
65	  ⇒  (f.c.app U).hom b = (φ_2'.app (f⁻¹ U)).hom ((adj_f.unit.app _).app U b)
66	  ```
67	  Then by `(derivation' φ_2').d_app` applied to the adjunction-unit value, the result vanishes (the universal derivation kills adjunction-unit pullbacks since they come from `Y.presheaf`).
68	
69	**Mathlib leverage names** (confirmed in-file at L431–L437):
70	- `SheafOfModules.hom_ext` (`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Sheaf.lean` L53).
71	- `PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.isUniversal'.postcomp_injective` (`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Differentials/Presheaf.lean` L101).
72	- `PresheafOfModules.Derivation.d_app` (`.../Differentials/Presheaf.lean` L63).
73	- `PresheafOfModules.Derivation` `@[ext]` for pointwise equality.
74

20	* `MayerVietorisCore.lean`: MV LES core + Mathlib gap-fill.
21	* `MayerVietorisCover.lean`: 2-affine cover MV + cover-totality bridges +

36	
37	/-- Iter-054: basic-open cover from a spanning subset of `Γ(C.left, U)`.
38	
39	Given a subset `s ⊆ Γ(C.left, U)`, this constructs the family of basic opens
40	`s → Opens` mapping each `f ∈ s` to `C.left.basicOpen f`. When `s` spans the
41	unit ideal of `Γ(C.left, U)` and `U` is affine, this family covers `U`
42	(see `basicOpenCover_supr_of_span_eq_top`); each member is itself an affine
43	open (see `basicOpenCover_isAffineOpen`).
44	
45	Iter-054 is a thin Mathlib-API wrapper (parameterised by the project's
46	`C : Over (Spec (.of k))` convention) so iter-055+ instantiation work for
47	`HasAffineCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C)` can call the constructor +
48	supremum + affine-membership directly.
49	
50	Mirrors the iter-047 foundational parameterised Čech infrastructure pattern:
51	package thin Mathlib wrappers as named project-local declarations so the
52	substantive multi-iteration work downstream focuses purely on content
53	(Koszul acyclicity + Čech-vs-derived comparison) rather than re-deriving
54	infrastructure at every call site. -/
55	noncomputable def basicOpenCover
56	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
57	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
58	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) :
59	    s → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat :=
60	  fun f => C.left.basicOpen (f : Γ(C.left, U))
61	
62	/-- Iter-054: supremum equality for the basic-open cover from generators
63	spanning the unit ideal of an affine open.
64	
65	Given `(hU : IsAffineOpen U)` and `(hs : Ideal.span s = ⊤)`, the basic-open
66	cover `basicOpenCover s` covers `U` exactly: `⨆ i, basicOpenCover s i = U`.
67	This is a thin wrapper around Mathlib's `IsAffineOpen.iSup_basicOpen_eq_self_iff`
68	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/AffineScheme.lean` L898).
69	
70	Used by iter-055+ when constructing the `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C)`
71	instance: the existential's third conjunct `(⨆ i, 𝒰 i = U)` is exactly what
72	this theorem produces. -/
73	theorem basicOpenCover_supr_of_span_eq_top
74	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
75	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat} (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
76	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (hs : Ideal.span s = ⊤) :
77	    ⨆ i, basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s i = U := by
78	  unfold basicOpenCover
79	  exact hU.iSup_basicOpen_eq_self_iff.mpr hs
80

800	          dsimp [g_FC]
801	          congr
802	          funext i_1
803	          erw [Limits.Pi.map_id, CategoryTheory.op_id, CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id,
804	            CategoryTheory.Category.comp_id]
805	      -- Step 3: the kernel of `π` at degree `i` is the product of the factors
806	      -- indexed by `x : Fin (i+1) → s` that are *not* in the image of
807	      -- `Fin (i+1) → ↑s₀`.  The kernel complex is acyclic in positive degrees
808	      -- because it is a Čech complex of a cover that is a refinement of a cover
809	      -- with a terminal object (the extra-degeneracy argument applies to the
810	      -- kernel once `s` is reduced modulo `↑s₀`).  Alternatively, one can use
811	      -- the sheaf-theoretic fact that Čech cohomology depends only on the
812	      -- generated sieve, and both `s` and `s₀` generate the same sieve `U`.
813	      --
814	      -- Step 4: from the short exact sequence of complexes
815	      -- `0 → ker(π) → K → K₀ → 0` and exactness of `K₀` at degree `n`, the

147	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
148	    (algebraMap k (C.left.presheaf.obj U)) = (kToSection C U).hom :=
149	  rfl
150	
151	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (4): the structure-morphism algebra map is
152	natural in `U`. -/
153	lemma kToSection_naturality (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
154	    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) :
155	    kToSection C U ≫ C.left.presheaf.map f = kToSection C V := by
156	  simp only [kToSection, Category.assoc, ← C.left.presheaf.map_comp]
157	  rfl
158	
159	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (5): corollary of (4) at the level of
160	`algebraMap`. -/
161	lemma algebraMap_naturality (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
162	    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) (r : k) :
163	    (C.left.presheaf.map f).hom (algebraMap k (C.left.presheaf.obj U) r)
164	      = algebraMap k (C.left.presheaf.obj V) r := by
165	  rw [algebraMap_eq_kToSection, algebraMap_eq_kToSection]
166	  exact congrArg (fun (g : (CommRingCat.of k) ⟶ _) => g.hom r)
167	    (kToSection_naturality (C := C) f)

1	# Čech / Koszul / acyclic-cover precedent audit (iter-059)
2	
3	## Project context
4	
5	Iter-053–058 set up the infrastructure for a forthcoming Čech-acyclicity
6	proof on basic-open covers:
7	
8	- `class HasAffineCechAcyclicCover F` (iter-053) — for every affine `U`, the
9	  existence of a cover `𝒰` of `U` such that `[IsCechAcyclicCover F 𝒰]` and
10	  `[HasCechToHModuleIso F 𝒰]`.
11	- `basicOpenCover s : s → Opens` and its supremum / membership /
12	  pairwise- / Finset-`inf'`-intersection identifications
13	  (iter-054 → iter-058).
14	- `class IsCechAcyclicCover F 𝒰` (iter-048) — positive-degree
15	  Čech cohomology subsingleton.
16	- `class HasCechToHModuleIso F 𝒰` (iter-050) — `Nonempty`-wrapped
17	  `∀ n, cechCohomology n ≃ₗ[k] HModule' k F n (⨆𝒰)`.
18	
19	The project now needs, for `F := toModuleKSheaf C`,
20	
21	1. `IsCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C) (basicOpenCover s)` whenever
22	   `Ideal.span (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) = ⊤`.
23	2. `HasCechToHModuleIso (toModuleKSheaf C) (basicOpenCover s)` for the same
24	   `s`.
25	
26	Before committing iter-059+ to the project-local construction, this analogy
27	call audits Mathlib for prior art.
28	
29	## What `cechComplexFunctor` produces
30	
31	`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/Cech.lean:65`
32	
33	```lean
34	noncomputable def cechComplexFunctor : (Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A) ⥤ CochainComplex A ℕ :=
35	  FormalCoproduct.cochainComplexFunctor (FormalCoproduct.mk _ U).cech
36	```
37	
38	Unfolding the constituents:
39	
40	- `FormalCoproduct.cochainComplexFunctor E = cosimplicialObjectFunctor E
41	  ⋙ AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex A`
42	  (`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/Cech.lean:53`)
43	- `cosimplicialObjectFunctor E = evalOp C A ⋙ (whiskeringLeft _ _ _).obj
44	  E.rightOp` (same file, L43)
45	- `(FormalCoproduct.mk _ U).cech.obj ⦋n⦌ = U.power (Fin (n+1))`, whose
46	  underlying object in `C` is `∏ᶜ (U ∘ i)` indexed by
47	  `i : Fin (n + 1) → ι` (`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Limits/FormalCoproducts/Cech.lean:34,186`)
48	
49	In our setting `C = (Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ`, `A = ModuleCat k`,
50	`P = (sheafToPresheaf _ _).obj F`, finite products in `Opens` are infima.
51	The cochain at degree `n` is therefore exactly
52	
53	```
54	∏_{x : Fin (n+1) → ι} F(⨅ k : Fin (n+1), 𝒰 (x k))
55	```
56	
57	— a `Module k` product (not a Π-type) inside `ModuleCat.{u} k`.
58	The differential `d^n` is the standard alternating sum
59	`∑_{j : Fin (n+2)} (-1)^j · δ_j` (Mathlib's `alternatingCofaceMapComplex`,
60	`Mathlib/AlgebraicTopology/AlternatingFaceMapComplex.lean:67`).
61	
62	**Crucial caveat**: this is the *unaugmented* Čech complex. There is no
63	`F(U) → ∏_i F(𝒰 i)` term sitting in degree `-1`. Standard Čech-acyclicity
64	for affine covers is usually stated about the *augmented* complex (Stacks
65	01EJ / 03B1), so the project either has to build the augmentation
66	explicitly, or carry the equivalent reasoning at the level of the
67	unaugmented homology (using the sheaf axiom for degree 0).
68	
69	## What Mathlib has
70	
71	### Precedent: `CategoryTheory.cechComplexFunctor` (definition only)
72	
73	`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/Cech.lean:65` — file path
74	**confirmed unchanged** since iter-047; the import statement in
75	`AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK.lean:784,796,813`
76	still resolves.
77	
78	```lean
79	noncomputable def cechComplexFunctor : (Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A) ⥤ CochainComplex A ℕ
80	```
81	
82	Used by iter-012 / iter-047 to define `cechCochain_OC`, `cechCochain`,
83	`cechCohomology`. No comparison theorem to derived functors is bundled
84	with the construction.
85	
86	### Precedent: `CategoryTheory.SimplicialObject.Augmented.ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv`
87	
88	`Mathlib/AlgebraicTopology/ExtraDegeneracy.lean:328`
89	
90	```lean
91	noncomputable def homotopyEquiv [Preadditive C] [HasZeroObject C]
92	    {X : SimplicialObject.Augmented C} (ed : ExtraDegeneracy X) :
93	    HomotopyEquiv (AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingFaceMapComplex.obj (drop.obj X))
94	      ((ChainComplex.single₀ C).obj (point.obj X))
95	```
96	
97	Augmented-simplicial-with-extra-degeneracy ⇒ alternating face map complex
98	is homotopy equivalent to the constant complex on the augmentation. This
99	is the **engine** for proving that a Čech complex over a "good" cover is
100	null-homotopic in positive degrees: the work that would otherwise be the
101	alternating-sum-cancellation argument is encapsulated here. Note this is
102	on `AlternatingFaceMapComplex` (chain complex) rather than
103	`alternatingCofaceMapComplex` (cochain complex) — a dual will need to be
104	spelled out (or extracted by op).
105	
106	### Precedent: `CategoryTheory.Limits.FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech`
107	
108	`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Limits/FormalCoproducts/ExtraDegeneracy.lean:92`
109	
110	```lean
111	noncomputable def extraDegeneracyCech {i₀ : U.I} (d : T ⟶ U.obj i₀) :
112	    (U.cech.augmentOfIsTerminal (isTerminalIncl _ hT)).ExtraDegeneracy
113	```
114	
115	Specialises `homotopyEquiv` to the formal-coproduct Čech object: given a
116	"vertex" — a section `T ⟶ U.obj i₀` of one of the cover terms from the
117	terminal — the augmented Čech object has an extra degeneracy. **This is
118	what fires after localizing at a single `f_i`**: in `D(f_i)`, the basic
119	open `D(f_i)` is a unit, so the affine `Spec(R[1/f_i]) = D(f_i)` is the
120	terminal of the cover-of-`D(f_i)` slice, and the inclusion `D(f_i) ⊂
121	D(f_i)` (i.e. the cover's `i₀ = i` member) supplies the section. Modulo
122	op-direction bookkeeping, this gives "Čech complex over `D(f_i)` is
123	null-homotopic in positive degrees" almost for free.

125	### Precedent: `exact_of_isLocalized_span`
126	
127	`Mathlib/RingTheory/LocalProperties/Exactness.lean:173`
128	
129	```lean
130	lemma exact_of_isLocalized_span (H : ∀ r : s, Function.Exact
131	    (map (.powers r.1) (f r) (g r) F) (map (.powers r.1) (g r) (h r) G)) :
132	    Function.Exact F G
133	```
134	
135	(plus its sibling at L211, `exact_of_localized_span`, which directly
136	takes `f : M →ₗ N` without prequalifying localization data.)
137	
138	Hypothesis shape: for each generator `r ∈ s` with `span s = ⊤`,
139	`map (.powers r) (f r) (g r) F` (the induced map between localizations)
140	is composed with `map (.powers r) (g r) (h r) G` to be exact. Conclusion:
141	`F` and `G` are exact unlocalized.
142	
143	**This is the spanning-set Koszul-style local exactness lemma**. The
144	iter-057 correction (noting the project's iter-056 docstring referenced a
145	non-existent `exact_of_localized_span` without the "is" prefix) was an
146	overcorrection: both `exact_of_localized_span` and
147	`exact_of_isLocalized_span` exist; the former is the user-facing direct
148	form, the latter is the explicit-localization-data form. The iter-058
149	report should clarify both forms exist and which one the iter-059+ work
150	will actually call.

248	## Cited references
249	
250	### Stacks Project §01ED (Čech cohomology)
251	
252	The acyclicity-on-affine proof in the Stacks Project (tag 01ED, sections
253	on Čech cohomology of quasicoherent sheaves on affine schemes) goes as
254	follows, *without* invoking Koszul:
255	
256	1. For an affine `U = Spec R`, a finite cover `𝒰 = (D(f_i))_i` with
257	   `(f_i)` generating the unit ideal of `R`, and a quasi-coherent `F`
258	   with `M = F(U)`, the augmented Čech complex
259	   `0 → M → ∏_i M[1/f_i] → ∏_{ij} M[1/f_i f_j] → ...` is exact.
260	
261	2. **Proof**: exactness is local on `D(f_i)` for each `i`. Localising at
262	   `f_i`, the augmented Čech complex over `D(f_i)` has `D(f_i)` as a cover
263	   member (`f_i` is a unit in `R[1/f_i]`), giving an extra degeneracy
264	   via "inclusion of the vertex". By the standard extra-degeneracy
265	   homotopy, the localised augmented complex is contractible, hence
266	   exact.
267	
268	3. By "exact on each open of a spanning cover ⇒ exact globally" (which
269	   is `exact_of_isLocalized_span` in Mathlib), the original augmented
270	   complex is exact.
271	
272	This route is the most economical for our setting: it requires *no*
273	Koszul-complex machinery; Mathlib's `ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv`
274	encapsulates the alternating-sum cancellation; and the local-to-global
275	step is exactly `exact_of_isLocalized_span`.
276	
277	### Stacks Project §03OU (Čech-to-cohomology spectral sequence)
278	
279	The general comparison `H^p(C^•(𝒰, F)) → H^p(U, F)` (Čech-to-derived
280	edge map) extends to a spectral sequence
281	`E^{p,q}_2 = Ȟ^p(𝒰, H^q(F)) ⇒ H^{p+q}(U, F)`. When the cover is acyclic
282	(all `H^q(intersection, F) = 0` for `q > 0`), the spectral sequence
283	degenerates and the edge map is an isomorphism. Mathlib does not have
284	this spectral sequence in any form. **For the project, the most
285	economical substitute** is to construct the edge map directly (without
286	the spectral sequence) by:
287	
288	- Producing the augmented Čech complex `C^• : 0 → F(U) → C^0 → C^1 → ...`
289	  on the affine. By step (1)–(3) above, this is acyclic, hence a
290	  resolution of `F(U)`.
291	- Sheaf-evaluating against an injective resolution of `F` (Mathlib does
292	  not provide injective sheaves in general, so this is the bottleneck).
293	- Identifying the resulting double-complex bipartite homology with
294	  `Ext`.
295	
296	Honest Lean blueprint estimate for the comparison: ~200-400 LOC.
297

313	### What the project must provide
314	
315	#### Branch A — Čech acyclicity (~150-250 LOC)
316	
317	For each affine `U` and spanning `s ⊆ Γ(C.left, U)`:
318	
319	1. **Augmentation lemma** (~30-50 LOC). Define the augmentation
320	   `F(U) → ∏_i F(𝒰 i)` as the natural sheaf-restriction morphism, and
321	   produce an *augmented* cochain complex extending iter-047's
322	   `Scheme.cechCochain`. This is structural; no Mathlib wrapper exists.
323	
324	2. **Localized identification** (~60-100 LOC). For each `f ∈ s`, identify
325	   the localization (at `f`) of the degree-`n` Čech cochain
326	   `∏_x F(⨅ k, 𝒰 (x k))` with the degree-`n` cochain of the
327	   "cover-of-`D(f)`" Čech complex. The key inputs are
328	   `IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen` (each intersection
329	   identifies as `Localization.Away` of `Γ(U)`) and
330	   `IsLocalizedModule.map_exact` (chain-level localization-preserves-exactness).
331	
332	3. **Extra-degeneracy invocation** (~30-60 LOC). The cover-of-`D(f)`
333	   Čech complex contains `D(f) = D(f ∩ f)` as a member, so applying
334	   `FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech` (or its op-dual for cochain
335	   complexes) gives an extra degeneracy on the augmented complex. By
336	   `ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv`, the augmented cochain complex over
337	   `D(f)` is homotopy equivalent to the constant complex on `F(D(f))`,
338	   hence the (un-augmented) Čech complex over `D(f)` is exact in
339	   positive degrees.
340	
341	4. **Local-to-global** (~30-40 LOC). Per-degree, apply
342	   `exact_of_isLocalized_span s hs ...` with the per-`f` local exactness
343	   from step 3 to lift to exactness of the unlocalized differential.
344	   Repackage as `IsCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C) (basicOpenCover s)`.
345	
346	#### Branch B — Čech-vs-derived comparison (~150-200 LOC)
347	
348	This is the substantial branch. Possibilities, ranked by economy:
349	
350	(i) **Direct comparison via augmented complex as resolution** (~150-200
351	LOC). After Branch A, the augmented Čech complex is exact, hence a
352	resolution of `F(U)`. Use `Abelian.Ext`'s functoriality (Mathlib has
353	`Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv₀` and adjacent API) to identify the homology of
354	`Hom(augmented Čech, F)` with `Ext^n(R, F)`.
355	
356	(ii) **Inductive Mayer-Vietoris** (~250-400 LOC). For finite covers,
357	unwind the cover one basic open at a time using the iter-014 LES.
358	Requires inductive structure and ad-hoc finite-cover reduction. **Not
359	recommended** — does more work and produces a less reusable artefact.
360	
361	(iii) **Spectral sequence formalisation** (~600+ LOC). Build Stacks 03OU
362	in Lean. **Strongly not recommended** — out of scope for the genus
363	proof.
364	
365	**Recommendation**: pursue (i). Total Branch B estimate ~150-200 LOC.

374	## Recommendation
375	
376	**Do not bypass the Čech-vs-derived comparison.** No Mathlib precedent
377	exists for "acyclic cover ⇒ Čech = Ext", but the constituent pieces —
378	`ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv`, `extraDegeneracyCech`,
379	`exact_of_isLocalized_span`, `isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen` — combine
380	into the standard Stacks 01ED proof of Čech acyclicity (Branch A) with
381	modest project-local glue (~150-250 LOC). The Čech-vs-derived comparison
382	(Branch B) is the real obligation; spending ~150-200 LOC on the direct
383	"augmented Čech complex is a resolution" route gives the cleanest
384	deliverable.
385	
386	**The alternating-sum-cancellation argument the directive feared having
387	to reproduce is NOT needed**: `ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv` encapsulates
388	it. The project work is the *bookkeeping* of mapping the localized Čech
389	complex to a Čech complex over `D(f)` (Branch A step 2), not the cancellation
390	itself.
391	
392	**Naming alignment**: all paths and names cited by the project (iter-047
393	import, iter-053–058 wrappers) remain valid. Recommend the iter-059
394	plan-agent update the iter-056 docstring at
395	`AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/MayerVietoris.lean:1441` to say
396	`exact_of_isLocalized_span` (or `exact_of_localized_span`, depending on
397	which form the project ends up calling — both exist).
398	
399	## Caveats
400	
401	- **Op direction**: Mathlib's `extraDegeneracyCech` builds on
402	  `SimplicialObject.Augmented`, giving an augmented *simplicial* object
403	  with extra degeneracy. The corresponding alternating face map complex
404	  is a *chain* complex. Our Čech complex is a *cochain* complex (built
405	  from an *augmented cosimplicial* object via
406	  `alternatingCofaceMapComplex`). The op-duality is straightforward but
407	  takes ~20-30 LOC of routine repackaging.
408	- **Augmentation construction**: the augmentation `F(U) → ∏_i F(𝒰 i)` is
409	  natural in `F`, so it should be derivable from the universal property
410	  of the formal-coproduct construction. But the project will need to
411	  spell this out — Mathlib does not currently package the augmentation
412	  alongside `cechComplexFunctor`.
413	- **Quasi-coherence assumption**: Stacks 01ED is for *quasi-coherent*
414	  sheaves on affine schemes, where the augmented Čech complex is exact
415	  *as a complex of `Γ(U)`-modules*. Our `toModuleKSheaf` is not the
416	  structure sheaf as an `O_C`-module — it's the structure sheaf as a
417	  *constant `k`-module*. The Stacks proof carries over because the
418	  alternating-sum cancellation is purely *combinatorial* (lives at the
419	  level of the simplicial structure, not the algebra structure). But the
420	  blueprint should note this distinction so future readers don't
421	  conflate "quasi-coherent sheaves of `O_C`-modules" with "sheaves of
422	  `k`-modules underlying the structure sheaf".
423	- **`Subsingleton` vs `IsZero`**: project's `IsCechAcyclicCover` carries
424	  a `Subsingleton (cechCohomology n)` field. Mathlib's
425	  `subsingleton_H_of_isZero` (`SheafCohomology/Basic.lean:74`) reduces
426	  via `IsZero`. The project may want to expose both forms; the
427	  `Subsingleton` form is the chainable one (matches iter-040's
428	  `IsAffineHModuleVanishing` consumer).
429

342	/-- Iter-066 helper: in `ModuleCat k`, a product projection induced by an
343	injection of index sets is a split epi.
344	
345	Given an injection `f : β → α` and a family `M : α → ModuleCat k`, the natural
346	projection `∏ᶜ_a M a ⟶ ∏ᶜ_b M (f b)` defined as `Pi.lift (fun b => Pi.π M (f b))`
347	(which sends a tuple indexed by `α` to its `image f` components, reindexed by `β`)
348	admits a section in `ModuleCat k`. The section is the "extend by zero" map:
349	given a tuple indexed by `β`, fill in the components in `image f` (using the
350	unique preimage given by injectivity of `f`) and set the remaining components
351	to `0`.
352	
353	This is the categorical fact underlying `h_π_split` in
354	`basicOpenCover_isCechAcyclicCover_toModuleKSheaf`: the cochain-complex map
355	`π : K ⟶ K₀` is at each degree of this form (the `↑s₀ ↪ s` inclusion induces a
356	postcomposition map `(Fin (i+1) → ↑s₀) → (Fin (i+1) → s)` which is injective by
357	injectivity of the inclusion, hence the induced product projection on cochain
358	factors is split).
359	
360	The helper is stated for a generic injection so it can be reused for both the
361	`s` substep (`h_π_split` proper) and the slice-cover variants. -/
362	noncomputable def splitEpi_pi_lift_of_injective {k : Type u} [Field k]
363	    {α β : Type u} (M : α → ModuleCat.{u} k)
364	    (f : β → α) (hf : Function.Injective f) :
365	    SplitEpi (Pi.lift (fun b : β => Pi.π M (f b))) where
366	  section_ := by
367	    classical
368	    refine Pi.lift (fun a => ?_)
369	    by_cases h : ∃ b, f b = a
370	    · exact h.choose_spec ▸ Pi.π (fun b => M (f b)) h.choose
371	    · exact 0
372	  id := by
373	    classical
374	    apply Pi.hom_ext
375	    intro b
376	    rw [Category.assoc, Pi.lift_π, Category.id_comp, Pi.lift_π]
377	    have h : ∃ b', f b' = f b := ⟨b, rfl⟩
378	    rw [dif_pos h]
379	    -- The transport `h.choose_spec ▸ Pi.π _ h.choose = Pi.π _ b`.
380	    -- By injectivity of `f`, `h.choose = b`, so the transport collapses.
381	    -- We use the helper lemma below.
382	    have key : ∀ {b b' : β} (heq : f b' = f b),
383	        heq ▸ (Pi.π (fun b'' => M (f b'')) b' :
384	            (∏ᶜ fun b'' => M (f b'')) ⟶ M (f b')) =
385	          (Pi.π (fun b'' => M (f b'')) b :
386	            (∏ᶜ fun b'' => M (f b'')) ⟶ M (f b)) := by
387	      intro b b' heq
388	      have hbb : b' = b := hf heq
389	      subst hbb
390	      rfl
391	    exact key h.choose_spec
392	
393	/-- Iter-060 helper: bridge from `ExactAt n` on the Čech cochain complex to
394	`Subsingleton (cechCohomology n)`.
395	
396	The chain is two steps: `HomologicalComplex.ExactAt.isZero_homology` packages an
397	`ExactAt n` hypothesis into `IsZero ((cechCochain ...).homology n)`, and
398	`ModuleCat.subsingleton_of_isZero` then upgrades `IsZero` on a `ModuleCat`
399	object to `Subsingleton` on its underlying carrier type.
400	
401	This is the structural skeleton common to *every* `IsCechAcyclicCover` instance
402	proof: the actual mathematical content lives in establishing
403	`(cechCochain C F 𝒰).ExactAt n` for each `n > 0`. -/
404	theorem cechCohomology_subsingleton_of_cechCochain_exactAt
405	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
406	    {F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)}
407	    {ι : Type u} {𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
408	    {n : ℕ} (h : (cechCochain C F 𝒰).ExactAt n) :
409	    Subsingleton (cechCohomology C F 𝒰 n) :=
410	  ModuleCat.subsingleton_of_isZero h.isZero_homology
411	
412	/-- Iter-060 (Phase A step 6 / Path 2 — **substantive Čech acyclicity attempt**):
413	the basic-open cover of an affine open `U` by a spanning subset `s ⊆ Γ(C.left, U)`
414	gives a Čech-acyclic cover for the `ModuleCat k`-valued structure sheaf
415	`toModuleKSheaf C`. This is the Stacks 01ED Čech-acyclicity theorem for affine
416	schemes in our setting.
417	
418	**Mathematical content (Stacks 01ED route).** For each positive degree `n`,
419	exactness of the Čech cochain at degree `n` is a *local* property on the
420	spanning set `s`. Localising the Čech complex at any `f ∈ s` exhibits the
421	slice over `D(f)` as a Čech complex of an affine cover whose terminal vertex
422	`D(f) → D(f)` supplies an *extra degeneracy* (Mathlib's
423	`FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech`). By
424	`SimplicialObject.Augmented.ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv` the augmented
425	chain complex of the slice is homotopy equivalent to the constant complex on
426	the augmentation, hence exact in positive degrees. Re-globalising via
427	`exact_of_isLocalized_span` (Mathlib `RingTheory/LocalProperties/Exactness.lean`
428	L173) yields exactness of the *unlocalised* Čech complex at degree `n`.
429	
430	The structural skeleton is:
431	* reduce `Subsingleton (cechCohomology n)` to `ExactAt (cechCochain) n` via
432	  `cechCohomology_subsingleton_of_cechCochain_exactAt`;
433	* the substantive `ExactAt n` is decomposed into three substeps tracked by
434	  separate `sorry`s, each annotated with the missing computational step.

437	directive; each sorry names a substep. The substeps are:
438	  (a) **local exactness at `D(f)`** (extra-degeneracy invocation): given the
439	      cover-of-`D(f)` slice, build the augmented Čech simplicial object,

301	  ∑ i : Fin (n + 2), (-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) • X.δ i

304	    objD X n =

306	          n).unop := by

312	  simp only [d_eq_unop_d, ← unop_comp, AlternatingFaceMapComplex.d_squared, unop_zero]
313	
314	/-- The alternating coface map complex, on objects -/
315	def obj : CochainComplex C ℕ :=
316	  CochainComplex.of (fun n => X.obj ⦋n⦌) (objD X) (d_squared X)
317	
318	variable {X} {Y}

322	def map (f : X ⟶ Y) : obj X ⟶ obj Y :=
323	  CochainComplex.ofHom _ _ _ _ _ _ (fun n => f.app ⦋n⦌) fun n => by
324	    dsimp
325	    rw [comp_sum, sum_comp]
326	    refine Finset.sum_congr rfl fun x _ => ?_
327	    rw [comp_zsmul, zsmul_comp]
328	    congr 1
329	    symm

453	theorem basicOpenCover_isCechAcyclicCover_toModuleKSheaf
454	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
455	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat} (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
456	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (hs : Ideal.span s = ⊤) :
457	    IsCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C)
458	        (basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s) where
459	  subsingleton_cechCohomology n hn := by
460	    -- Step 0 (structural): reduce `Subsingleton (cechCohomology n)` to
461	    -- `ExactAt n` of the underlying Čech cochain complex via the iter-060
462	    -- helper `cechCohomology_subsingleton_of_cechCochain_exactAt`.
463	    refine cechCohomology_subsingleton_of_cechCochain_exactAt (n := n) ?_
464	    -- The goal is now
465	    --   `(cechCochain C (toModuleKSheaf C) (basicOpenCover s)).ExactAt n`.
466	    -- Iter-061 decomposition: the Stacks 01ED extra-degeneracy + local-to-global
467	    -- argument has three substeps tracked by separate labelled `sorry`s.
468	    --
469	    -- ===========================================================================
470	    -- Substep (a) — Extra-degeneracy on the slice cover of `D(f)`.
471	    -- ===========================================================================
472	    -- For each `f ∈ s`, the slice cover of `D(f)` by the family
473	    --   `{basicOpenCover s f' ⊓ D(f)}_{f' ∈ s}`
474	    -- contains `D(f) ⊓ D(f) = D(f)` itself (at index `f' = f`), so the
475	    -- terminal vertex maps to `D(f)` via the identity section
476	    -- `D(f) → D(f)`. By `FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech`
477	    -- (`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Limits/FormalCoproducts/ExtraDegeneracy.lean` L92),
478	    -- the augmented Čech simplicial object on this slice has an
479	    -- `ExtraDegeneracy`. By `SimplicialObject.Augmented.ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv`
480	    -- (`Mathlib/AlgebraicTopology/ExtraDegeneracy.lean` L328), the associated
481	    -- alternating-face-map chain complex is homotopy-equivalent to the
482	    -- constant complex on the augmentation. Op-passage through
483	    -- `CochainComplex.opEquivalence` then yields cochain-level exactness in
484	    -- positive degrees. We state substep (a) as a concrete `ExactAt n`
485	    -- claim on the slice cover Čech cochain complex:
486	    have h_a : ∀ (f : s),
487	        (cechCochain C (toModuleKSheaf C)
488	            (fun (f' : s) => basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s f' ⊓
489	              C.left.basicOpen (f : Γ(C.left, U)))).ExactAt n := by
490	      intro f
491	      -- The terminal vertex of the slice cover is at `f' = f`:
492	      --   `basicOpenCover s f ⊓ C.left.basicOpen f.1 = D(f) ⊓ D(f) = D(f)`.
493	      -- Plug into `FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech` with `i₀ := f`,
494	      -- extract `homotopyEquiv`, op-pass, read off `ExactAt n` for `0 < n`.
495	      sorry  -- substep (a) infrastructure: augmented Čech simplicial object
496	             -- + extraDegeneracyCech + homotopyEquiv + CochainComplex.opEquivalence
497	    -- ===========================================================================
498	    -- Substep (b) — Localised-Čech identification.
499	    -- ===========================================================================
500	    -- For each `f ∈ s`, the slice cover Čech cochain complex above is
501	    -- isomorphic (in `ModuleCat k`) to the localisation at `f` of the
502	    -- original Čech cochain complex `K`. Cochain-factor-wise:
503	    --   `(slice cover).X^n = ∏_x F(⨅ k, basicOpenCover s (x k) ⊓ D(f))`
504	    --                      = `∏_x F(D(f · ∏ k, (x k).1))`              (Stacks identity)
505	    --                      = `∏_x Localization.Away (f · ∏ k, (x k).1) Γ(C.left, U)`
506	    --                      = `∏_x Γ(C.left, ⨅ k, basicOpenCover s (x k))[1/f]`
507	    --                      = `K.X^n [1/f]`.
508	    -- The first equality is iter-057 (`basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_eq_basicOpen_prod`)
509	    -- applied to the augmented index `{f} ∪ image(x)`. The second is
510	    -- iter-059 (`basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_isLocalization`). The third is
511	    -- the universal property of localisation (transitivity of localisation:

513	    -- The differential identification uses `IsLocalizedModule.map_exact`
514	    -- (`Mathlib/Algebra/Module/LocalizedModule/Exact.lean` L56) to transport
515	    -- the cochain-level identification to the slice differential.
516	    --
517	    -- ===========================================================================
518	    -- Substep (c) — Local-to-global via `exact_of_isLocalized_span`.
519	    -- ===========================================================================
520	    -- The combined data from substeps (a) and (b) — per-`f` exactness at

# AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean

## Iter-074 Lane 2 — close `h_diff_pi_smul_f` (L996)

### Attempt 1 (executed)
- **Approach:** Execute the in-file S1–S8 reduction recipe with defensive
  `try`-wrappers preserving file compilation under the broken build env.
  Concrete chain:
  ```
  intro r y
  try funext j
  try simp only [scK₀, K₀, cechCochain, cechComplexFunctor,
    FormalCoproduct.cochainComplexFunctor,
    FormalCoproduct.cosimplicialObjectFunctor,
    FormalCoproduct.evalOp, AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex,
    AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.obj,
    AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD,
    HomologicalComplex.sc, ShortComplex.f, CochainComplex.of,
    CochainComplex.ofHom, ComplexShape.up]
  try simp only [Pi.smul_apply, Finset.sum_apply, Finset.smul_sum]
  sorry
  ```
- **Result:** IN PROGRESS — chain committed to the file (L1057–L1101); the
  final `sorry` remains because the per-summand R-linearity step
  (S7 in the recipe) requires concrete `Finset.sum_congr rfl` +
  `algebraMap_naturality` rewriting that cannot be validated without LSP.
- **Env state:** build env confirmed broken in this session.
  `lean_diagnostic_messages` returns `success: false`; `lean_goal`
  returns `goals: null`; `lean_multi_attempt` returns empty goals
  uniformly (LSP is not analyzing the file). Per the iter-074 PROGRESS
  note: *"Commit to a credible attempt anyway"* — done.

### Why the residual sorry stayed
Without LSP feedback I cannot:
1. Confirm that the `simp only [...]` 5-layer functor-stack unfolding
   actually exposes the alternating-sum normal form. Some of the
   `@[simp]`-tagged equations in Mathlib's `AlternatingCofaceMapComplex`
   namespace may not commute with this particular order of unfoldings;
   the iter-073 commentary already flagged this as a risk
   (*"the mechanical execution needs `dsimp` through the 5-layer functor
   stack and careful handling of `letI`-introduced module instances"*).
2. Confirm that the `Pi.smul_apply` / `Finset.sum_apply` distribution
   step lands on a goal where the per-summand reduction via
   `algebraMap_naturality` is syntactically applicable. The R-action on
   `∀ i, Z₁ i` is `Pi.module` with each component using a different
   ring-hom `(presheaf.map (V_i ≤ U).op).hom`; the index of the chosen
   ring-hom (`a0 = ⟨0, _⟩` in `h_mod_pi₁`) introduces an arbitrary-choice
   that must be reconciled with the index `j ∘ δ_k.toOrderHom` on the
   output side.

The chain is wrapped in `try` so that if any individual step fails to
elaborate, the file's compilation is preserved (per the iter-073
defensive convention). When the env is repaired, the next prover can
either:
- step through the `try`-wrapped tactics one by one to identify which
  step makes progress, then drop the failed `try` wrappers; or
- replace the `simp only` step with explicit `show ... = ...` / `change`
  reformulations using the names from analogies/cech-koszul-precedent.md
  to expose the alternating-sum directly.

### Mathematical content of the residual obligation (for iter-075)
After the chain executes (assuming it elaborates without failure), the
goal at the final `sorry` is the per-component R-linearity claim:
```
∑ k : Fin (n + 2), ((-1)^k.val •
  ((presheaf.map (V_j ≤ V_{j∘δ_k}).op).hom (((r • y) (j ∘ δ_k.toOrderHom))))) =
r •
∑ k : Fin (n + 2), ((-1)^k.val •
  ((presheaf.map (V_j ≤ V_{j∘δ_k}).op).hom (y (j ∘ δ_k.toOrderHom))))
```
The LHS-`(r • y) i` unfolds via `h_mod_pi₁` to
`(presheaf.map (V_i ≤ U).op).hom r * y i`. The restriction is a ring-hom
so it splits as
`(presheaf.map (V_j ≤ V_i).op).hom ((presheaf.map (V_i ≤ U).op).hom r) *
 (presheaf.map (V_j ≤ V_i).op).hom (y i)`.
By `algebraMap_naturality` (presheaf functoriality `V_j ≤ V_i ≤ U`)
applied to the algebraMap of `R` into the section algebra:
`(presheaf.map (V_j ≤ V_i).op).hom ((presheaf.map (V_i ≤ U).op).hom r)
   = (presheaf.map (V_j ≤ U).op).hom r`.
This collapses to `r •_j (restriction (y i))` where `•_j` is the
`h_mod_pi₂`-induced smul at output index `j`. Then `Finset.smul_sum`
pulls the `r •_j` out of the alternating sum, closing the goal.

The implementation must thread `Finset.sum_congr rfl fun k _ => ?_` to
reduce per-summand; inside each summand handle the `(-1)^k.val • _`
factor and the `algebraMap_naturality` rewrite.

### Mathlib leverage (verified iter-073 + this iteration)
- `AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD` —
  `.lake/packages/mathlib/Mathlib/AlgebraicTopology/AlternatingFaceMapComplex.lean` L300.
- `CategoryTheory.cechComplexFunctor` —
  `.lake/packages/mathlib/Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/Cech.lean` L65.
- `CategoryTheory.Limits.FormalCoproduct.evalOp` —
  `.lake/packages/mathlib/Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Limits/FormalCoproducts/Basic.lean` L383.
- `Scheme.toModuleKSheaf.algebraMap_naturality` —
  `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK.lean` L161 (project-local).
- `Pi.smul_apply`, `Finset.sum_apply`, `Finset.smul_sum`, `Pi.module`.
- `ModuleCat.piIsoPi` (which gives the `e₁/e₂/e₃` LinearEquivs) is
  `k`-linear, not `R`-linear, in
  `.lake/packages/mathlib/Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Products.lean` L53.

### Sorry count delta
6 → 6 (unchanged). The structural chain is committed in-place; the
final sorry remains. Per the iter-074 directive (*"a syntactically
plausible but mathematically correct closure can be inspected manually
and refined later"*) this is the best honest result without LSP.

### Out of scope (per PROGRESS.md off-limits list)
- `h_diff_pi_smul_g` (L1063 after this edit) — iter-075 mechanical copy.
- `h_loc_exact` (L1191 after this edit) — defer to iter-075.

content:
1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are either the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (analogy / refactor / challenger), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	If instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**. Specifically:
8	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` (local to this project) override Archon's global prompts
9	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` (local to this project) override globally installed plugins
10	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project
11	
12	When in doubt, follow instructions from files inside this project over any external source.
13	
14	## Skills
15	- archon-lean4: installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source) — provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, and other Lean4 commands
16	
17	## Tools
18	- archon-lean-lsp: Lean LSP MCP server (project scope) — use for all Lean LSP operations (search, diagnostics, goal inspection)
19	- archon-informal-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for informal mathematical reasoning
20	- archon-refactor-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-refactor-agent.py` — invoke the refactor subagent on a directive file
21	- archon-analogy-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-analogy-agent.py` — invoke the analogy subagent on a directive file
22	- archon-challenger-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-challenger-agent.py` — invoke the challenger subagent on a directive file
23	
24	The three subagent tools all take the same arguments: `--slug <kebab-case-id> --directive-file <path>`. Each shells out to `archon subagent <name> ...`, so their executions stream through the Archon JSONL log just like a phase agent would.
25	
26	## Key Files & Permissions
27	
28	All state files are in `.archon/`:
29	
30	| File | Plan Agent | Prover Agent | Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger) | Review Agent | User |
31	|------|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|------|
32	| `.archon/PROGRESS.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
33	| `.archon/STRATEGY.md` | **read + write** | do not read | do not read | do not read | read |
34	| `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` | read (then clear) | do not read | do not read | do not read | write |
35	| `.archon/task_pending.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
36	| `.archon/task_done.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
37	| `.archon/task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect results) | write (own file only) | write (`<role>-<slug>.md`) | read only | read |
38	| `.archon/proof-journal/` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
39	| `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
40	| `archon-protected.yaml` | **read** | **read** | **read** (refactor may rename file path only) | read | **write** |
41	| `.lean` files | do not edit | write (own file only, frozen protected signatures) | refactor: write (all files; protected decls may be moved but not renamed/re-signed); analogy/challenger: read-only on project source | do not edit | write (via comments) |
42	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints, structure) | do not edit | do not edit | **write** (markers only: `\leanok`, `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections) | read |
43	| `Challenges/<Name>.lean` | do not edit | fill sorries (when assigned) | challenger: **write** | do not edit | read |
44	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | do not access | analogy: **write** | do not access | read |
45	
46	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow, run by hand by the mathematician.** The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. The plan agent invokes the refactor subagent by writing a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/` and passing its path to `archon-refactor-agent.py --directive-file`. This keeps each loop-driven refactor independently logged and avoids any chance of stale-directive reuse. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`, etc.) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise and prune them out as you rewrite.
47	
48	## Protected Declarations
49	
50	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Every agent must consult it before editing a `.lean` file. Rules:
51	
52	- **Plan / prover / review agents**: read-only on protected signatures. You may fill proof bodies, but not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
53	- **Refactor agent**: may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (keeping name + signature verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. Refactor agents may never rename, re-type, delete, or re-sign a protected declaration.
54	- The `archon-protected.yaml` file can only be edited by the user, no declaration can be added or removed by any agent. The only allowed modification is updating the file path of an existing protected declaration when its location was changed.
55	
56	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
57	
58	The blueprint uses two active markers.
59	
60	- `\leanok` — inside a statement block when the declaration is formalized with at least a `sorry`; inside a proof block when the proof is fully closed with no `sorry`.
61	- `\mathlibok` — inside a statement block when the declaration already exists in Mathlib and the Archon side is a re-export/alias; no Archon proof obligation remains.
62	- No marker — the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
63	
64	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between the prover and review phases each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves — let the script do it.
65	
66	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations remain the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
67	
68	## User Interaction
69	
70	Users provide hints in two places:
71	
72	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The plan agent reads this and translates hints into concrete objectives. Provers never read this file.
73	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
74	
75	## Agent Roles
76	
77	### Plan Agent
78	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for your full instructions
79	- Read `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` — incorporate hints, then clear them after acting
80	- Read `.archon/task_results/` — collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md`
81	- Optionally invoke the **subagents** (`analogy`, `refactor`, `challenger`) by shelling out to the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, **before** writing prover objectives. See `.archon/prompts/plan.md` § "Subagent delegation" for canonical ordering, the directive format for each subagent, and the exact invocation pattern.
82	- Archive any subagent report you receive to `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<role>-<slug>-report.md` so the dashboard can render it.
83	- Write `.archon/PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round
84	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` (except for marker updates) and `\lean{...}` hints for the provers
85	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself
86	
87	### Prover Agent
88	- Read `.archon/PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
89	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/`:
90	  - autoformalize → `.archon/prompts/prover-autoformalize.md`
91	  - prover → `.archon/prompts/prover-prover.md`
92	  - polish → `.archon/prompts/prover-polish.md`
93	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md`
94	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — **never edit another agent's file**
95	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your `.lean` file for file-specific hints
96	- **Do NOT edit blueprint chapters.** Marker updates are the review agent's responsibility. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker.
97	
98	### Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger)
99	
100	These are **not** part of the autonomous loop's standing phases. The plan agent invokes them on demand by running the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, with `--slug <slug> --directive-file <path>`. Each subagent reads only the files the directive points it at — never `PROGRESS.md`, `STRATEGY.md`, or other plan-agent state — and writes a self-contained report.
101	
102	- **`refactor`** — read `.archon/prompts/refactor.md`. Executes structural changes (definitions, signatures, file splits, imports). Inserts `sorry` at broken proof sites; never fills proofs. Writes report to `.archon/task_results/refactor-<slug>.md`.
103	- **`analogy`** — read `.archon/prompts/analogy.md`. Reads project files, finds Mathlib precedents, writes a design-rationale analysis. Persistent output at `analogies/<slug>.md`; report at `.archon/task_results/analogy-<slug>.md`. Read-only on project source.
104	- **`challenger`** — read `.archon/prompts/challenger.md`. Adds discriminating sanity-check theorems with `sorry` to `Challenges/<Name>.lean`. Provers fill them later. Report at `.archon/task_results/challenger-<slug>.md`. Read-only on target files.
105	
106	The plan agent always reads the full report file after a subagent returns and may then update `STRATEGY.md` / `PROGRESS.md` based on the findings. None of the subagents spawn other subagents.
107	
108	### Review Agent
109	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for your full instructions
110	- Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data
111	- Write session journal to `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
112	- Update `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` with overall progress
113	- Maintain the **semantic** blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE: ...` annotations, stale `\notready` removal — in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch `\leanok`; it's handled by the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase that ran before you.
114	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify PROGRESS.md
115	
116	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
117	
118	Two non-agent steps run automatically each iteration to reduce the burden on the agents above:
119	
120	- **Pre-compactors** (`compact-strategy`, `compact-task-pending`, `compact-task-done`, `compact-project-status`): run before plan / review, rewriting oversized state files in place while preserving every actionable detail. Configured under `compaction.*` in `.archon/config.json`. Skipped when files are below threshold.
121	- **`sync_leanok`**: runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status. Replaces what used to be a multi-page review-agent task.
122	
123	Both write inner-git commits (`archon[NNN/precompact/...]`, `archon[NNN/marker-sync]`) so their output is auditable and revertable.

29	- Lane 1 — `Differentials.lean`: 7→7. Added 3-step partial-tactic prefix to `_structure.h_zero` (L395), exposing `⟨(isUniversal' φ_g').desc d_target⟩ ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).map β = 0`. Steps 4-7 remain.

31	- Lane 3 — `Jacobian.lean`: 1→1. Refactored — dite `if h : genus C = 0 then 𝟙_ _ else (jacobianWitness C).J` removed; body now `(jacobianWitness C).J` unconditionally; the four protected instances project the witness fields directly (term-mode). No new axioms; protected signatures unchanged.
32	- Lane 4 — `AbelJacobi.lean`: 1→0. **Phase E closes by reduction**: all three theorems (`ofCurve`, `comp_ofCurve`, `exists_unique_ofCurve_comp`) now project unconditionally through `((jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P)`; the genus-0 rigidity content previously held by the bare sorry is absorbed into Phase C's `nonempty_jacobianWitness`.
